title: hydnocarpus kunstleri var. tomentosus
intro: Hydnocarpus kunstleri var. tomentosus, also known as the Burmese stinking nut or stinking nut, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Flacourtiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The extracts from this plant have been shown to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and alleviating pain. The herbal preparation of Hydnocarpus kunstleri var. tomentosus typically involves drying and powdering the plant's seeds or leaves, which can be used in various forms such as capsules, teas, or ointments.
